morphology.summary: A compact semantic-control capsule for guarantee; morphology is not the focus of this seed entry.

etymology.summary: Etymology for guarantee is intentionally deferred in this semantic-control seed capsule.

semantic_drift.summary: Semantic-control usage focuses on present operational meaning rather than historical drift for guarantee.

present_usage.summary: A strong assurance that an outcome will happen or be made good.

senses.definition: A strong assurance that an outcome will happen or be made good.

examples.text: The word guarantee changes what claims the agent may safely make.

cultural_frame.summary: guarantee appears in communication where tone, risk, evidence, or commitment must be controlled.

frames.label: semantic control
frames.description: Frames guarantee as a term whose pragmatic force can change the safe output path.

pragmatics.summary: Guarantee language raises expectations and can create commitment beyond available evidence.

stances.label: control-sensitive
stances.description: Guarantee language raises expectations and can create commitment beyond available evidence.

expansion.paragraph: guarantee is represented here as a semantic-control term: its present usage and pragmatic force help decide whether an agent should ask, check evidence, avoid overclaiming, or revise before export.
